My expertise lies in implementing Site Reliability Engineering principles, focusing on automation, monitoring, and incident response within cloud-native environments. I have hands-on experience with core CNCF technologies like Kubernetes for orchestration, Prometheus and Grafana for robust observability, and GitOps workflows for declarative infrastructure management. I thrive on optimizing system performance, enhancing reliability, and fostering a culture of continuous improvement, always eager to explore the cutting edge of the cloud-native landscape

person.name: Abdullah Saeed Qureshi
person.bio: It was quite a decision to take on what to pursue after successfully completing my High School and acing A-Levels. I kept hearing about the growing hype around microservices, Kubernetes, and my father, Saud Qureshi, who works at IBM, encouraged me to explore it further. What began as simple curiosity quickly turned into a serious commitment. I stayed up late many nights, pushing through exhaustion, confusion, and countless errors while trying to understand complex Kubernetes concepts. In just over four months, all that effort paid off when I became a Kubestronaut at the age of 19! The journey so far wasn't easy, but it strengthened my discipline and deepened my passion for cloud-native technologies. Today, I continue to build on that foundation by improving my programming skills, exploring modern infrastructure and software development, and diving into emerging areas such as AI and Agentic AI.

person.name: Adarsh R
person.bio: I have over 15 years of experience in Linux administration, DevOps, containerization, security, and cloud technologies. My expertise includes deploying and managing Rancher and applications across self-managed and cloud-managed Kubernetes platforms like RKE1, RKE2, EKS, AKS, and GKE. Passionate about cloud-native technologies, I continuously explore innovations in this space to enhance operational efficiency in modern infrastructure.

person.name: Dipesh Rawat
person.bio: Dipesh Rawat is an experienced software developer with expertise in Kubernetes, cloud-native development, and DevOps practices. His work encompasses CI/CD implementations and the creation of scalable, secure solutions. As an active contributor to the Kubernetes community, he is dedicated to advancing cloud technologies and sharing his knowledge to promote community growth and innovation.

person.name: Adhila Eramangalath Aydrose
person.bio: I am a cloud-native professional specializing in the Kubernetes ecosystem, with a deep focus on building secure, scalable infrastructure. My technical foundation is rooted in hands-on experience with CI/CD, Helm, and Infrastructure as Code (IaC), and I hold a Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist (CKS) designation. Beyond the command line, I am an active contributor to the tech community, where I share insights on Docker and Python through my technical blog and by organizing local Meetups. I am passionate about mentoring others and helping organizations navigate the complexities of modern cloud architecture.

person.name: Elkana Mbiti
person.bio: I'm a self-taught Python and Golang software and cloud engineer. Early in my journey, I simply believed software 'just worked.' It wasn't until I was immersed in the world of DevOps that I quickly realized how layered and interconnected software systems are – software supported by software, supported by more software... a fascinating complexity. This realization fueled continuous learning and self-improvement, leading to certifications in multiple cloud platforms, Kubernetes, and software testing. Outside of work, I'm a nature enthusiast with a strong interest in computer networking, storage, and systems reliability, especially within the cloud native solutions ecosystem.

person.name: Chen Xue
person.bio: I am currently focusing on Kubernetes and CNCF technologies, with hands-on experience in IT infrastructure, CI/CD, and platform engineering.

person.name: Shivay Lamba
person.bio: Shivay Lamba is a software developer specializing in DevOps, Machine Learning and Full Stack Development.  He is an Open Source Enthusiast and has been part of various programs like Google Code In and Google Summer of Code as a Mentor and has also been a MLH Fellow.  He is actively involved in community work as well. He is a TensorflowJS SIG member, Mentor in OpenMined and CNCF Service Mesh Community, SODA Foundation

person.name: Valentin Hristev
person.bio: I'm a Cloud Native consultant and trainer with over two decades of experience. I specialize in keeping cloud environments clear and storm-free, helping companies implement cutting-edge solutions. Passionate about innovation and embracing challenges, I dedicate myself to empowering individuals through continuous learning. I also actively participate in non-profit organizations and community events. Outside of work, I enjoy spending time with my dog and exploring my passion for cars. My dedication to technology and personal growth is complemented by my drive to master life's bumpy and uncertain courses.

person.name: Lương Trung Kiên
person.bio: System admin mainly but just forced switch to python developer recently xD

person.name: Paula Kennedy
person.bio: Paula is part of the organising committee for Kubernetes Community Days UK, DevOpsDays London, FastFlowConf, and the London Platform User Group. In her day job, Paula is Co-Founder and Chief Operating Officer of Syntasso. She is passionate about community, diversity and inclusion, enjoys speaking at events like KubeCon and also cohosted the first Platform Engineering colocated day at KubeCon Paris. In her spare time, Paula enjoys walking her dog and attempting to run half-marathons.

person.name: Samaksh Chandravanshi
person.bio: Samaksh Chandravanshi is a Senior Cloud DevOps Engineer based in Bengaluru, India, with over 4 years of experience architecting and managing cloud-native solutions, primarily on Microsoft Azure. Specializing in Kubernetes, Istio, Terraform, and modern CI/CD best practices, Samaksh is passionate about optimizing infrastructure through automation and robust security—holding CKA, CKAD, CKS, and Azure expert certifications. A regular contributor to the CNCF and DevOps community, Samaksh shares expertise and insights on cloud-native technologies and container orchestration through in-depth articles on Medium at https://medium.com/@samaksh.clouddevops. With a proven track record in implementing GitOps, service mesh, and scalable monitoring solutions, Samaksh is dedicated to helping teams achieve velocity, reliability, and cost efficiency in their cloud journeys.

I’m passionate about Kubernetes and containerized workloads, and I’ve led the design and delivery of large-scale, enterprise-grade platforms for critical customers worldwide. Beyond engineering, I actively share knowledge and contribute to building resilient, scalable systems that empower teams to innovate with confidence.

My expertise spans Kubernetes, Linux, Solaris, and Windows Server, and I bring extensive experience in system engineering, technical support, software integration, and complex infrastructure deployments.

person.name: Unni P

I am also passionate about sharing my knowledge with others and have contributed technical articles on Hashnode to help others in the tech community. I believe that continuous learning and sharing knowledge is essential in the technology field. In addition to my technical expertise, I strongly advocate for the principles of open-source, collaboration, and shared responsibility. Blog link: https://iamunnip.hashnode.dev/

person.name: Adhi Sutandi

My toolkit includes key CNCF technologies like Kubernetes, Flux, Helm, Prometheus, and provisioning tools like OpenTofu and Sops. I share my occasional insights and technical guides on my blog: https://medium.com/@sanadhis.
